mf_core.label: MF Core
mf_core.primaryProductLabel: Agency Long-Term Fixed (Fannie DUS / Freddie Conventional)
mf_core.rationale: Core multifamily stabilized assets underwrite best for agency (Fannie/Freddie). Non-recourse, long-term fixed rates, and supplemental potential make agency the clear primary.

mf_core_plus.label: MF Core-Plus
mf_core_plus.rationale: Core-plus assets benefit from agency execution with supplemental loan capacity in Y3-5 to fund light value-add capex without a full refi.

mf_value_add.label: MF Value-Add
mf_value_add.rationale: Value-add properties need bridge flexibility during renovation (IO, draws, no immediate DSCR test), then permanent fixed-rate agency financing at stabilization to lock in long-term capital.

mf_deep_value_add.label: MF Deep Value-Add
mf_deep_value_add.rationale: Deep value-add requires higher leverage to fund heavy capex. Senior bridge to 70% LTC + mezz to 80-85% LTC achieves the capital needed. Mezz at 12-14% pay + PIK.

mf_distressed.label: MF Distressed
mf_distressed.rationale: Distressed assets carry performance and covenant risk that conventional lenders won't underwrite. Private credit accepts the risk premium in exchange for higher pricing and partial recourse.

mf_lease_up.label: MF Lease-Up
mf_lease_up.rationale: Lease-up assets fund at partial proceeds initially; earnout provisions release remaining proceeds as occupancy and DSCR milestones are met, reducing initial lender risk.

mf_ground_up.label: MF BTS Ground-Up
mf_ground_up.rationale: Ground-up construction requires a construction loan with interest reserve and draw schedule. At certificate of occupancy, the exit is typically a refi to permanent or sale at stabilization.

sfr_fix_flip.label: SFR Fix-and-Flip
sfr_fix_flip.rationale: Fix-and-flip requires fast close, tolerance for under-construction collateral, and short duration. Hard money lenders specialize in this and can fund in days vs weeks for conventional.

sfr_brrrr.label: SFR BRRRR
sfr_brrrr.rationale: BRRRR requires acquisition flexibility (hard money or cash), followed by a DSCR refi after 6-month seasoning to extract equity and recycle capital into the next deal.

sfr_hold.label: SFR Hold (Scattered)
sfr_hold.rationale: Stabilized SFR rental holds access conventional financing with favorable 30-year amortization. DSCR qualification eliminates personal income constraints for investors.

sfr_portfolio.label: SFR Portfolio Aggregation
sfr_portfolio.rationale: Portfolios of 5+ SFR properties aggregate well into blanket loans offering simpler management, better pricing, and cross-collateralization benefits vs property-by-property financing.

retail_nnn_core.label: Retail NNN Core
retail_nnn_core.rationale: Single-tenant NNN properties with credit tenants underwrite best for CMBS. Match loan term to lease expiration to maximize value and avoid refinance risk.

retail_grocery.label: Retail Grocery-Anchored
retail_grocery.rationale: Grocery-anchored centers are among the most financeable retail formats. CMBS or life company execution achieves lowest cost of capital with long-term fixed rates.

retail_value_add.label: Retail Value-Add Reposition
retail_value_add.rationale: Repositioning retail requires execution flexibility and tolerance for below-stabilized occupancy during lease-up. Bridge provides the time and funding to re-tenant and reposition.

office_core.label: Office Core

structure.note: Office underwritten conservatively post-2023; lenders require lower leverage

office_core.rationale: Core office with long-term credit tenants can still access institutional fixed-rate capital, but at conservative LTVs (55-65%) reflecting post-2023 office market re-pricing.

office_value_add.label: Office Value-Add
office_value_add.rationale: Office value-add requires TI/LC holdbacks to fund re-leasing costs. Bridge lenders underwrite looser DSCR floors during vacancy to fund the value-creation period.

industrial_core.label: Industrial Core
industrial_core.rationale: Industrial is currently the most financeable asset class. Long-term credit tenants, strong NOI growth, and low vacancy underwrite well for institutional fixed-rate capital.

industrial_value_add.label: Industrial Value-Add
industrial_value_add.rationale: Industrial value-add through re-leasing or renovation benefits from bridge flexibility, then refinances to fixed at stabilization.

mf_cls.label: MF Class A / Workforce Housing
mf_cls.rationale: Class A ground-up or workforce housing assets are prime candidates for HUD 221(d)(4) if timeline allows (12-18mo processing). Highest leverage (80%+), longest amortization, non-recourse. Agency supplemental eligible after stabilization.

sfr_new_construction.label: SFR Build-to-Rent (BTR)
sfr_new_construction.rationale: BTR communities require construction financing (65% LTC) with a path to portfolio blanket loan or DSCR refi once stabilized (90%+ occupancy). Full recourse during construction; non-recourse available at perm.

sfr_str.label: SFR Short-Term Rental (STR)
sfr_str.rationale: Short-term rental properties qualify for DSCR loans underwritten on ADR/RevPAR projections (or trailing 12-month actuals). Key: confirm lender accepts STR income; some require 12mo ownership seasoning.

retail_power_center.label: Retail Power Center / Big Box
retail_power_center.rationale: Power centers require CMBS or life co execution with anchor tenant underwriting. Lenders stress vacancy at 20%+ given big-box exposure. Target 60% LTV; defeasance prepay standard.

office_medical.label: Medical Office (MOB)
office_medical.rationale: Medical office is lender-preferred due to healthcare tenancy (long leases, high switchover costs). CMBS and life companies active at 65% LTV, 7-10yr fixed. Health system credit tenants unlock best pricing.

office_flex.label: Flex Office / Coworking / R&D
office_flex.rationale: Flex and coworking spaces face skeptical underwriting from traditional lenders. Bridge with earnout unlocks proceeds as occupancy milestones are hit. Target 80% occupancy for earnout release; then pursue bank term loan.

industrial_cold_storage.label: Industrial Cold Storage
industrial_cold_storage.rationale: Cold storage commands best-in-class pricing from life companies due to mission-critical tenant exposure and limited supply pipeline. 60% LTV standard; mechanical replacement reserves required. Net lease to credit food/3PL tenants preferred.

industrial_last_mile.label: Industrial Last-Mile / Urban Logistics
industrial_last_mile.rationale: Infill last-mile industrial sees strong cap rate compression from e-commerce demand. Bridge with earnout supports lease-up; NNN lease execution at 90%+ unlocks CMBS or agency-adjacent perm refi.

hospitality_core.label: Hospitality Core (Stabilized Flag)
hospitality_core.rationale: Stabilized flag properties qualify for CMBS execution at 60-65% LTV. FF&E reserves are lender-required and must be modeled in sources and uses.

hospitality_limited_service.label: Hospitality Limited-Service / Select-Service
hospitality_limited_service.rationale: Select-service hotels (Marriott Courtyard, Hilton Garden Inn, etc.) are bankable with franchise agreement in place. Regional banks prefer full recourse at 5yr balloon. CMBS available for flag properties with strong RevPAR.

hospitality_extended_stay.label: Hospitality Extended Stay

structure.underwritingNote: Mix of nightly ADR and monthly lease income; lenders apply 30-40% haircut to monthly income

hospitality_extended_stay.rationale: Extended stay properties (WoodSpring, Candlewood, etc.) underwrite as hybrid hotel/residential. CMBS lenders apply income haircuts to monthly stays; target 62.5% LTV. Brand affiliation strongly preferred.

default.label: General Investment
default.rationale: Without a specific strategy detection, bridge financing provides maximum flexibility to preserve optionality during the initial hold period.
